response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with broad web, mobile, and some systems-level background (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services), but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no background in the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, tamper detection) or L2/L3 switch development. His embedded exposure is peripheral rather than firmware-focused, making him a poor fit despite strong general engineering credentials.
strengths: Long, diverse software engineering career with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some systems-level experience: C/C++ IDE components, low-latency asynchronous services, Linux
Proven ability to learn and work across many stacks and domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a mandatory requirement for this position
No Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or LAN-switch/networking-protocol experience
Career is centered on web/full-stack product work, far from embedded security development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Strong general engineering background with some C/C++ and Linux
